title: Sabulina viscosa
taxonomy: Sabulina viscosa is classified within the family of flowering plants known as Aizoaceae, commonly referred to as the ice plant family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic studies. The genus Sabulina is considered a part of the subfamily Ruschioideae, which comprises succulent plants native to the Cape region of South Africa. Members of the Ruschioideae subfamily are known for their unique adaptations to arid environments, such as thick, fleshy leaves that store water. Sabulina viscosa, like other members of the Aizoaceae family, has evolved to thrive in these conditions.

intro: Sabulina viscosa, also known as sticky bindweed, is a perennial climbing plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sabulina viscosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sabulina viscosa is often used to make topical preparations such as creams, ointments, and infusions, as well as oral tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
